prompt: Why does the wording of a prompt matter so much to the quality of a model's output?
choices: The model only sees the text you send it, so anything it needs to know must be in the prompt
Longer prompts always produce more accurate answers regardless of content
The model reads your project files and context automatically
Wording only affects tone, never correctness or format

prompt: Which statements about the system (or developer) message versus the user message are correct? Select all that apply.
choices: The system message sets durable rules and persona and persists across turns
The user message carries the specific, changing request
The user message takes priority over the system message
Safety policy and role are best placed in the system message

prompt: What is the main purpose of wrapping input data in delimiters (such as XML-style tags or triple quotes)?
choices: To separate your instructions from the data the model should process
To force the model to use a higher temperature
To translate the data into another language automatically

prompt: A PM asks a model to classify support tickets, but the script that consumes the output keeps breaking because the model replies with a friendly paragraph instead of a single label. Which prompt change most directly fixes this?
choices: Add a system instruction specifying the exact allowed labels and demanding one word with no explanation
Increase the temperature so answers vary more
Ask the model to be 'a bit more concise' without specifying a format
Send the request more times until a short answer appears
